Off Calendar Dec-02-2011 Continued To Feb-17-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-26-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEPHEN P. BANNER, TEL. NO. 434-3700 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. NATALIE LENTS OF SCHNADER & LEWIS LLP, TEL. NO. 364-6771 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT JOHN HWANG'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with ten days leave to amend. The order signed by Judge Goldsmith permits defendant to re-assert his argument on this demurrer that the claims alleged by plaintiff are derivative in nature. Because those claims are based on an alleged misconduct done to the corporation rather than plaintiff alone, they are derivative in nature and must be so plead. Defendant's statute of limitations argument lacks merit. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 = (302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-30-2011 Continued To Dec-16-11 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

Off Calendar Jun-20-2011 Continued To Sep-02-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-15-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT JOHN HWANG'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S VERIFIED COMPLAINT FOR FRAUD, BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY, CONVERSION, AN ACCOUNTING, IMPOSITION OF A CONSTRUCTIVE TRUST, AND FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF.

APPEARANCES: Stephen P. Brunner, Esq. of Angell, Brunner & Angell 434-3700 for pltf & opposing party; Natalie Bush Lents, Esq. of Schnader Harrison Segal & Lewis LLP 364-6771 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - LACK OF STANDING OVERRULED. UNCERTAINTY OVERRULED. FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD SPECIFICS OF MISREPRESENTATIONS ALLEGED. CONVERSION CAUSE OF ACTION SUSTAIN WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD SPECIFICS OF CLEAR OWNERSHIP INTEREST. In addition, the Court indicated that plaintiff has 30 days leave to amend. Defendant to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/EHG) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
